1→package project
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"fmt"
     5→	"os"
     6→	"os/exec"
     7→	"syscall"
     8→)
     9→
    10→type nextStepStep struct{}
    11→
    12→func (s *nextStepStep) Name() string { return "" } // no label, just prints
    13→
    14→func (s *nextStepStep) Done() (bool, error) { return false, nil }
    15→
    16→func (s *nextStepStep) Run() error {
    17→	fmt.Println()
    18→
    19→	if isInsideClaudeCode() {
    20→		fmt.Println("  You're ready. Run:")
    21→		fmt.Println("    /nurge:setup")
    22→		fmt.Println()
    23→		return nil
    24→	}
    25→
    26→	if isClaudeInstalled() {
    27→		fmt.Println("  Launching Claude Code...")
    28→		fmt.Println()
    29→		return launchClaude()
    30→	}
    31→
    32→	fmt.Println("  Almost there! Install Claude Code first:")
    33→	fmt.Println("    https://claude.ai/code")
    34→	fmt.Println()
    35→	fmt.Println("  Then run:")
    36→	fmt.Println("    claude -p \"/nurge:setup\"")
    37→	fmt.Println()
    38→	return nil
    39→}
    40→
    41→func isInsideClaudeCode() bool {
    42→	return os.Getenv("CLAUDE_CODE") != ""
    43→}
    44→
    45→func isClaudeInstalled() bool {
    46→	_, err := exec.LookPath("claude")
    47→	return err == nil
    48→}
    49→
    50→// launchClaude replaces the current process with claude in interactive mode.
    51→// SessionStart hooks handle the rest — banner shows status, prompt guides Claude.
    52→func launchClaude() error {
    53→	claudePath, err := exec.LookPath("claude")
    54→	if err != nil {
    55→		return fmt.Errorf("claude not found: %w", err)
    56→	}
    57→
    58→	// syscall.Exec replaces the nurge process with claude — clean handoff
    59→	return syscall.Exec(claudePath, []string{"claude"}, os.Environ())
    60→}
    61→

1→package email
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"database/sql"
     5→	"encoding/json"
     6→	"fmt"
     7→	"strings"
     8→	"time"
     9→
    10→	gm "google.golang.org/api/gmail/v1"
    11→)
    12→
    13→type RawMessage struct {
    14→	ID          string
    15→	Source      string
    16→	Direction   string
    17→	ThreadID    string
    18→	Subject     string
    19→	FromEmail   string
    20→	ToEmails    string // JSON array
    21→	CcEmails    string // JSON array
    22→	Date        string
    23→	Snippet     string
    24→	BodyPreview string
    25→	Labels      string // JSON array
    26→}
    27→
    28→type SyncResult struct {
    29→	Fetched  int
    30→	Stored   int
    31→	Cursor   string
    32→	Duration time.Duration
    33→}
    34→
    35→func Sync(srv *gm.Service, d *sql.DB) (*SyncResult, error) {
    36→	start := time.Now()
    37→	result := &SyncResult{}
    38→
    39→	var cursor string
    40→	d.QueryRow("SELECT sync_cursor FROM connectors WHERE id = 'gmail'").Scan(&cursor)
    41→
    42→	query := "in:sent"
    43→	if cursor == "" {
    44→		since := time.Now().AddDate(0, 0, -90).Format("2006/01/02")
    45→		query += " after:" + since
    46→	}
    47→
    48→	var pageToken string
    49→	for {
    50→		req := srv.Users.Messages.List("me").Q(query).MaxResults(100)
    51→		if pageToken != "" {
    52→			req = req.PageToken(pageToken)
    53→		}
    54→
    55→		resp, err := req.Do()
    56→		if err != nil {
    57→			return result, fmt.Errorf("listing messages: %w", err)
    58→		}
    59→
    60→		for _, msg := range resp.Messages {
    61→			result.Fetched++
    62→
    63→			if cursor != "" {
    64→				var exists int
    65→				d.QueryRow("SELECT 1 FROM emails WHERE id = ?", msg.Id).Scan(&exists)
    66→				if exists == 1 {
    67→					continue
    68→				}
    69→			}
    70→
    71→			full, err := srv.Users.Messages.Get("me", msg.Id).Format("metadata").
    72→				MetadataHeaders("Subject", "From", "To", "Cc", "Date").Do()
    73→			if err != nil {
    74→				fmt.Printf("  warning: skipping message %s: %v\n", msg.Id, err)
    75→				continue
    76→			}
    77→
    78→			email := parseMessage(full)
    79→			if err := StoreEmail(d, email); err != nil {
    80→				fmt.Printf("  warning: storing message %s: %v\n", msg.Id, err)
    81→				continue
    82→			}
    83→			result.Stored++
    84→		}
    85→
    86→		pageToken = resp.NextPageToken
    87→		if pageToken == "" {
    88→			break
    89→		}
    90→	}
    91→
    92→	result.Cursor = time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
    93→	result.Duration = time.Since(start)
    94→
    95→	d.Exec(
    96→		`INSERT INTO connectors (id, type, sync_cursor, last_sync_at, total_synced)
    97→		 VALUES ('gmail', 'email', ?, ?, ?)
    98→		 ON CONFLICT(id) DO UPDATE SET sync_cursor = ?, last_sync_at = ?, total_synced = total_synced + ?`,
    99→		result.Cursor, result.Cursor, result.Stored,
   100→		result.Cursor, result.Cursor, result.Stored,

    34→
    35→		if analyticsFormat == "json" {
    36→			enc := json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout)
    37→			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
    38→			return enc.Encode(summary)
    39→		}
    40→
    41→		fmt.Printf("  Enrollments: active=%d paused=%d completed=%d stopped=%d\n",
    42→			summary.ActiveEnrollments, summary.PausedEnrollments, summary.CompletedEnrollments, summary.StoppedEnrollments)
    43→		fmt.Printf("  Tasks: open=%d completed=%d canceled=%d\n",
    44→			summary.OpenTasks, summary.CompletedTasks, summary.CanceledTasks)
    45→		fmt.Printf("  Replies=%d | suppressions=%d\n", summary.ReplyCount, summary.SuppressionCount)
    46→
    47→		if len(summary.BySequence) > 0 {
    48→			fmt.Println("  By sequence:")
    49→			for _, item := range summary.BySequence {
    50→				fmt.Printf("    %s: active=%d paused=%d completed=%d stopped=%d replies=%d open=%d\n",
    51→					item.Name, item.ActiveEnrollments, item.PausedEnrollments, item.CompletedEnrollments, item.StoppedEnrollments, item.ReplyCount, item.OpenTasks)
    52→			}
    53→		}
    54→
    55→		if len(summary.ByMailbox) > 0 {
    56→			fmt.Println("  By mailbox today:")
    57→			for _, item := range summary.ByMailbox {
    58→				fmt.Printf("    %s: first-touch=%d follow-up=%d reply=%d active=%d paused=%d\n",
    59→					item.MailboxEmail, item.FirstTouchesSent, item.FollowUpsSent, item.RepliesSent, item.ActiveEnrollments, item.PausedEnrollments)
    60→			}
    61→		}
    62→
    63→		return nil
    64→	},
    65→}
    66→
    67→func init() {
    68→	analyticsCmd.PersistentFlags().StringVar(&analyticsFormat, "format", "", "Output format (json)")
    69→	analyticsCmd.AddCommand(analyticsSummaryCmd)
    70→	rootCmd.AddCommand(analyticsCmd)
    71→}

1→package email
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"encoding/json"
     6→	"fmt"
     7→	"os"
     8→
     9→	"golang.org/x/oauth2"
    10→	"golang.org/x/oauth2/google"
    11→	gm "google.golang.org/api/gmail/v1"
    12→	"google.golang.org/api/option"
    13→
    14→	"github.com/usenurge/nurgesh/internal/connector"
    15→)
    16→
    17→const defaultTokenPath = ".nurge/connectors/gmail/token.json"
    18→
    19→// NewClient creates an authenticated Gmail service from a saved token.
    20→func NewClient(tokenPath string) (*gm.Service, error) {
    21→	if tokenPath == "" {
    22→		tokenPath = defaultTokenPath
    23→	}
    24→
    25→	tokenData, err := os.ReadFile(tokenPath)
    26→	if err != nil {
    27→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("reading token: %w", err)
    28→	}
    29→
    30→	var token oauth2.Token
    31→	if err := json.Unmarshal(tokenData, &token); err != nil {
    32→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("parsing token: %w", err)
    33→	}
    34→
    35→	credsData, err := connector.LoadCredentials("gmail")
    36→	if err != nil {
    37→		return nil, err
    38→	}
    39→
    40→	oauthCfg, err := google.ConfigFromJSON(credsData,
    41→		gm.GmailReadonlyScope,
    42→		gm.GmailSendScope,
    43→		gm.GmailModifyScope,
    44→	)
    45→	if err != nil {
    46→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("parsing credentials: %w", err)
    47→	}
    48→
    49→	client := oauthCfg.Client(context.Background(), &token)
    50→	srv, err := gm.NewService(context.Background(), option.WithHTTPClient(client))
    51→	if err != nil {
    52→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating Gmail service: %w", err)
    53→	}
    54→
    55→	return srv, nil
    56→}

    63→func RecordSend(d *sql.DB, mailboxID, sendType string, now time.Time) error {
    64→	sendType = NormalizeSendType(sendType)
    65→	if sendType == SendTypeManual {
    66→		return nil
    67→	}
    68→	if now.IsZero() {
    69→		now = time.Now()
    70→	}
    71→
    72→	_, err := d.Exec(`
    73→		INSERT INTO mailbox_daily_usage (
    74→			mailbox_id, usage_day, first_touches_sent, follow_ups_sent, replies_sent
    75→		) VALUES (?, ?, 0, 0, 0)
    76→		ON CONFLICT(mailbox_id, usage_day) DO NOTHING`,
    77→		mailboxID, dayKey(now),
    78→	)
    79→	if err != nil {
    80→		return err
    81→	}
    82→
    83→	column := usageColumn(sendType)
    84→	if column != "" {
    85→		if _, err := d.Exec(
    86→			fmt.Sprintf(`UPDATE mailbox_daily_usage SET %s = %s + 1 WHERE mailbox_id = ? AND usage_day = ?`, column, column),
    87→			mailboxID, dayKey(now),
    88→		); err != nil {
    89→			return err
    90→		}
    91→	}
    92→
    93→	if _, err := d.Exec(`
    94→		INSERT INTO mailbox_send_events (id, mailbox_id, send_type, sent_at)
    95→		VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)`,
    96→		newID("mse"), mailboxID, sendType, now.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
    97→	); err != nil {
    98→		return err
    99→	}
   100→
   101→	_, err = d.Exec(`UPDATE mailboxes SET last_used_at = ?, updated_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?`, now.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339), mailboxID)
   102→	return err
   103→}
   104→
   105→func LogSendAttempt(d *sql.DB, opts SendAttemptOptions) error {
   106→	if opts.MailboxID == "" {
   107→		return fmt.Errorf("mailbox ID is required")
   108→	}
   109→	if strings.TrimSpace(opts.ContactEmail) == "" {
   110→		return fmt.Errorf("contact email is required")
   111→	}
   112→	if strings.TrimSpace(opts.Status) == "" {
   113→		return fmt.Errorf("send attempt status is required")
   114→	}
   115→	if opts.AttemptedAt.IsZero() {
   116→		opts.AttemptedAt = time.Now()
   117→	}
   118→
   119→	_, err := d.Exec(`
   120→		INSERT INTO send_attempts (
   121→			id, mailbox_id, enrollment_id, task_id, contact_email, send_type,
   122→			status, subject, error, message_id, provider_id, attempted_at
   123→		) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
   124→		newID("send"), opts.MailboxID, opts.EnrollmentID, opts.TaskID, strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(opts.ContactEmail)),
   125→		NormalizeSendType(opts.SendType), opts.Status, opts.Subject, opts.Error, opts.MessageID, opts.ProviderID,
   126→		opts.AttemptedAt.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
   127→	)
   128→	return err
   129→}
   130→
   131→func ListSendAttempts(d *sql.DB, limit int, status string) ([]SendAttempt, error) {
   132→	query := `
   133→		SELECT s.id, s.mailbox_id, m.email, s.enrollment_id, s.task_id, s.contact_email, s.send_type,
   134→			s.status, s.subject, s.error, s.message_id, s.provider_id, s.attempted_at
   135→		FROM send_attempts s
   136→		JOIN mailboxes m ON m.id = s.mailbox_id`
   137→	var args []any
   138→	if status != "" {
   139→		query += ` WHERE s.status = ?`
   140→		args = append(args, status)
   141→	}
   142→	query += ` ORDER BY s.attempted_at DESC`
   143→	if limit > 0 {
   144→		query += fmt.Sprintf(" LIMIT %d", limit)
   145→	}
   146→
   147→	rows, err := d.Query(query, args...)
   148→	if err != nil {
   149→		return nil, err
   150→	}

    10→func ListReplyQueue(d *sql.DB, limit int) ([]ReplyEvent, error) {
    11→	query := `
    12→		SELECT id, enrollment_id, mailbox_id, thread_id, message_id, contact_email, classification,
    13→			status, assigned_to, subject, snippet, draft_reply, handled_at, occurred_at
    14→		FROM reply_events
    15→		WHERE status = 'open'
    16→		ORDER BY occurred_at DESC`
    17→	if limit > 0 {
    18→		query += fmt.Sprintf(" LIMIT %d", limit)
    19→	}
    20→
    21→	rows, err := d.Query(query)
    22→	if err != nil {
    23→		return nil, err
    24→	}
    25→	defer rows.Close()
    26→
    27→	var items []ReplyEvent
    28→	for rows.Next() {
    29→		var item ReplyEvent
    30→		if err := rows.Scan(
    31→			&item.ID, &item.EnrollmentID, &item.MailboxID, &item.ThreadID, &item.MessageID, &item.ContactEmail,
    32→			&item.Classification, &item.Status, &item.AssignedTo, &item.Subject, &item.Snippet, &item.DraftReply,
    33→			&item.HandledAt, &item.OccurredAt,
    34→		); err != nil {
    35→			return nil, err
    36→		}
    37→		item.NeedsAction = item.Status == "open"
    38→		items = append(items, item)
    39→	}
    40→	return items, rows.Err()
    41→}
    42→
    43→func MarkReplyHandled(d *sql.DB, replyID, assignedTo string) error {
    44→	replyID = strings.TrimSpace(replyID)
    45→	if replyID == "" {
    46→		return fmt.Errorf("reply ID is required")
    47→	}
    48→	_, err := d.Exec(`
    49→		UPDATE reply_events
    50→		SET status = 'handled',
    51→			assigned_to = CASE WHEN ? = '' THEN assigned_to ELSE ? END,
    52→			handled_at = ?,
    53→			created_at = created_at
    54→		WHERE id = ?`,
    55→		assignedTo, assignedTo, time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339), replyID,
    56→	)
    57→	return err
    58→}
    59→
    60→func ReopenReply(d *sql.DB, replyID string) error {
    61→	replyID = strings.TrimSpace(replyID)
    62→	if replyID == "" {
    63→		return fmt.Errorf("reply ID is required")
    64→	}
    65→	_, err := d.Exec(`
    66→		UPDATE reply_events
    67→		SET status = 'open',
    68→			handled_at = ''
    69→		WHERE id = ?`,
    70→		replyID,
    71→	)
    72→	return err
    73→}
    74→
    75→func SaveReplyDraft(d *sql.DB, replyID, draftReply, assignedTo string) (*ReplyEvent, error) {
    76→	replyID = strings.TrimSpace(replyID)
    77→	if replyID == "" {
    78→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("reply ID is required")
    79→	}
    80→	_, err := d.Exec(`
    81→		UPDATE reply_events
    82→		SET draft_reply = ?,
    83→			assigned_to = CASE WHEN ? = '' THEN assigned_to ELSE ? END
    84→		WHERE id = ?`,
    85→		draftReply, assignedTo, assignedTo, replyID,
    86→	)
    87→	if err != nil {
    88→		return nil, err
    89→	}
    90→	return replyEventByID(d, replyID)
    91→}
    92→
    93→func replyEventByID(d *sql.DB, replyID string) (*ReplyEvent, error) {
    94→	row := d.QueryRow(`
    95→		SELECT id, enrollment_id, mailbox_id, thread_id, message_id, contact_email, classification,
    96→			status, assigned_to, subject, snippet, draft_reply, handled_at, occurred_at
    97→		FROM reply_events
    98→		WHERE id = ?`,
    99→		replyID,
   100→	)
   101→	var item ReplyEvent
   102→	if err := row.Scan(
   103→		&item.ID, &item.EnrollmentID, &item.MailboxID, &item.ThreadID, &item.MessageID, &item.ContactEmail,
   104→		&item.Classification, &item.Status, &item.AssignedTo, &item.Subject, &item.Snippet, &item.DraftReply,
   105→		&item.HandledAt, &item.OccurredAt,
   106→	); err != nil {
   107→		if err == sql.ErrNoRows {
   108→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reply event %q not found", replyID)
   109→		}
   110→		return nil, err
   111→	}
   112→	item.NeedsAction = item.Status == "open"
   113→	return &item, nil
   114→}

    76→		ON CONFLICT(mailbox_id, usage_day) DO NOTHING`,
    77→		mailboxID, dayKey(now),
    78→	)
    79→	if err != nil {
    80→		return err
    81→	}
    82→
    83→	column := usageColumn(sendType)
    84→	if column != "" {
    85→		if _, err := d.Exec(
    86→			fmt.Sprintf(`UPDATE mailbox_daily_usage SET %s = %s + 1 WHERE mailbox_id = ? AND usage_day = ?`, column, column),
    87→			mailboxID, dayKey(now),
    88→		); err != nil {
    89→			return err
    90→		}
    91→	}
    92→
    93→	if _, err := d.Exec(`
    94→		INSERT INTO mailbox_send_events (id, mailbox_id, send_type, sent_at)
    95→		VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)`,
    96→		newID("mse"), mailboxID, sendType, now.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
    97→	); err != nil {
    98→		return err
    99→	}
   100→
   101→	_, err = d.Exec(`UPDATE mailboxes SET last_used_at = ?, updated_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?`, now.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339), mailboxID)
   102→	return err
   103→}

   105→func LogSendAttempt(d *sql.DB, opts SendAttemptOptions) error {
   106→	if opts.MailboxID == "" {
   107→		return fmt.Errorf("mailbox ID is required")
   108→	}
   109→	if strings.TrimSpace(opts.ContactEmail) == "" {
   110→		return fmt.Errorf("contact email is required")
   111→	}
   112→	if strings.TrimSpace(opts.Status) == "" {
   113→		return fmt.Errorf("send attempt status is required")
   114→	}
   115→	if opts.AttemptedAt.IsZero() {
   116→		opts.AttemptedAt = time.Now()
   117→	}
   118→
   119→	_, err := d.Exec(`
   120→		INSERT INTO send_attempts (
   121→			id, mailbox_id, enrollment_id, task_id, contact_email, send_type,
   122→			status, subject, error, message_id, provider_id, attempted_at
   123→		) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
   124→		newID("send"), opts.MailboxID, opts.EnrollmentID, opts.TaskID, strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(opts.ContactEmail)),
   125→		NormalizeSendType(opts.SendType), opts.Status, opts.Subject, opts.Error, opts.MessageID, opts.ProviderID,
   126→		opts.AttemptedAt.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
   127→	)
   128→	return err
   129→}
   130→
   131→func ListSendAttempts(d *sql.DB, limit int, status string) ([]SendAttempt, error) {
   132→	query := `
   133→		SELECT s.id, s.mailbox_id, m.email, s.enrollment_id, s.task_id, s.contact_email, s.send_type,
   134→			s.status, s.subject, s.error, s.message_id, s.provider_id, s.attempted_at
   135→		FROM send_attempts s
   136→		JOIN mailboxes m ON m.id = s.mailbox_id`
   137→	var args []any
   138→	if status != "" {
   139→		query += ` WHERE s.status = ?`
   140→		args = append(args, status)
   141→	}
   142→	query += ` ORDER BY s.attempted_at DESC`
   143→	if limit > 0 {
   144→		query += fmt.Sprintf(" LIMIT %d", limit)
   145→	}
   146→
   147→	rows, err := d.Query(query, args...)
   148→	if err != nil {
   149→		return nil, err
   150→	}
   151→	defer rows.Close()
   152→
   153→	var attempts []SendAttempt
   154→	for rows.Next() {
   155→		var item SendAttempt
   156→		if err := rows.Scan(
   157→			&item.ID, &item.MailboxID, &item.MailboxEmail, &item.EnrollmentID, &item.TaskID, &item.ContactEmail,
   158→			&item.SendType, &item.Status, &item.Subject, &item.Error, &item.MessageID, &item.ProviderID, &item.AttemptedAt,
   159→		); err != nil {
   160→			return nil, err
   161→		}
   162→		attempts = append(attempts, item)
   163→	}
   164→	return attempts, rows.Err()
   165→}

   167→func UpdateTaskSendState(d *sql.DB, taskID, sendStatus, messageID, providerID, lastError string) error {
   168→	sendStatus = strings.TrimSpace(sendStatus)
   169→	if sendStatus == "" {
   170→		sendStatus = TaskSendStatusPending
   171→	}
   172→	_, err := d.Exec(`
   173→		UPDATE follow_up_tasks
   174→		SET send_status = ?,
   175→			unipile_message_id = CASE WHEN ? = '' THEN unipile_message_id ELSE ? END,
   176→			provider_id = CASE WHEN ? = '' THEN provider_id ELSE ? END,
   177→			last_error = CASE WHEN ? = '' THEN last_error ELSE ? END,
   178→			updated_at = datetime('now')
   179→		WHERE id = ?`,
   180→		sendStatus,
   181→		messageID, messageID,
   182→		providerID, providerID,
   183→		lastError, lastError,
   184→		taskID,
   185→	)
   186→	return err
   187→}
   188→
   189→func UpsertDraftForTask(d execer, taskID, enrollmentID string, stepNumber int, subject, body, generatedBy, generatedContext string) error {
   190→	if taskID == "" || enrollmentID == "" {
   191→		return fmt.Errorf("task ID and enrollment ID are required")
   192→	}
   193→	_, err := d.Exec(`
   194→		INSERT INTO sequence_drafts (
   195→			id, enrollment_id, task_id, step_number, subject, body, channel, status, generated_by, generated_context
   196→		) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, 'email', 'pending', ?, ?)
   197→		ON CONFLICT(task_id) DO UPDATE SET
   198→			subject = excluded.subject,
   199→			body = excluded.body,
   200→			generated_by = excluded.generated_by,
   201→			generated_context = excluded.generated_context,
   202→			status = CASE
   203→				WHEN sequence_drafts.status = 'sent' THEN sequence_drafts.status
   204→				ELSE excluded.status
   205→			END,
   206→			updated_at = datetime('now')`,
   207→		newID("draft"), enrollmentID, taskID, stepNumber, subject, body, generatedBy, generatedContext,
   208→	)
   209→	return err
   210→}
   211→
   212→func DraftByTaskID(d *sql.DB, taskID string) (*SequenceDraft, error) {
   213→	row := d.QueryRow(`
   214→		SELECT id, enrollment_id, task_id, step_number, subject, body, channel, status, generated_by, generated_context
   215→		FROM sequence_drafts
   216→		WHERE task_id = ?`,
   217→		taskID,
   218→	)
   219→	var draft SequenceDraft
   220→	if err := row.Scan(
   221→		&draft.ID, &draft.EnrollmentID, &draft.TaskID, &draft.StepNumber, &draft.Subject, &draft.Body,
   222→		&draft.Channel, &draft.Status, &draft.GeneratedBy, &draft.GeneratedContext,
   223→	); err != nil {
   224→		if err == sql.ErrNoRows {
   225→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("draft for task %q not found", taskID)
   226→		}
   227→		return nil, err
   228→	}
   229→	return &draft, nil
   230→}
   231→
   232→func UpdateDraftStatusByTaskID(d execer, taskID, status string) error {
   233→	_, err := d.Exec(`UPDATE sequence_drafts SET status = ?, updated_at = datetime('now') WHERE task_id = ?`, status, taskID)
   234→	return err
   235→}

1→package session

     3→import (
     4→	"database/sql"

    11→// Learning represents a single insight captured from a Claude Code session.
    12→type Learning struct {
    13→	ID            int    `json:"id"`
    14→	Type          string `json:"type"`
    15→	Content       string `json:"content"`
    16→	SourceSession string `json:"source_session,omitempty"`
    17→	CreatedAt     string `json:"created_at"`
    18→}
    19→
    20→// SkillVersion represents a versioned snapshot of a skill.
    21→type SkillVersion struct {
    22→	ID            int    `json:"id"`
    23→	SkillName     string `json:"skill_name"`
    24→	Version       int    `json:"version"`
    25→	Content       string `json:"content"`
    26→	EmailCount    int    `json:"email_count"`
    27→	LearningCount int    `json:"learning_count"`
    28→	Active        bool   `json:"active"`
    29→	CreatedAt     string `json:"created_at"`
    30→}
    31→
    32→// SkillSummary is a brief view of a skill with its current version.
    33→type SkillSummary struct {
    34→	SkillName      string `json:"skill_name"`
    35→	ActiveVersion  int    `json:"active_version"`
    36→	TotalVersions  int    `json:"total_versions"`
    37→	LastUpdated    string `json:"last_updated"`
    38→}
    39→
    40→// SaveLearning inserts a new learning into the database.
    41→func SaveLearning(db *sql.DB, typ, content, sourceSession string) (int64, error) {
    42→	result, err := db.Exec(
    43→		`INSERT INTO learnings (type, content, source_session) VALUES (?, ?, ?)`,
    44→		typ, content, sourceSession,
    45→	)
    46→	if err != nil {
    47→		return 0, fmt.Errorf("saving learning: %w", err)
    48→	}
    49→	return result.LastInsertId()
    50→}
    51→
    52→// ListLearnings returns all learnings, optionally filtered by type.
    53→func ListLearnings(db *sql.DB, filterType string) ([]Learning, error) {
    54→	var rows *sql.Rows
    55→	var err error
    56→
    57→	if filterType != "" {
    58→		rows, err = db.Query(
    59→			`SELECT id, type, content, source_session, created_at FROM learnings WHERE type = ? ORDER BY created_at DESC`,
    60→			filterType,
    61→		)
    62→	} else {
    63→		rows, err = db.Query(
    64→			`SELECT id, type, content, source_session, created_at FROM learnings ORDER BY created_at DESC`,
    65→		)
    66→	}
    67→	if err != nil {
    68→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("listing learnings: %w", err)
    69→	}
    70→	defer rows.Close()
    71→
    72→	var learnings []Learning
    73→	for rows.Next() {
    74→		var l Learning
    75→		if err := rows.Scan(&l.ID, &l.Type, &l.Content, &l.SourceSession, &l.CreatedAt); err != nil {
    76→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("scanning learning: %w", err)
    77→		}
    78→		learnings = append(learnings, l)
    79→	}
    80→	return learnings, rows.Err()
    81→}
    82→
    83→// SaveSkillVersion saves a new version of a skill. It deactivates all previous
    84→// versions and marks the new one as active.
    85→func SaveSkillVersion(db *sql.DB, skillName, content string, emailCount, learningCount int) (int, error) {
    86→	tx, err := db.Begin()
    87→	if err != nil {
    88→		return 0, fmt.Errorf("beginning transaction: %w", err)
    89→	}
    90→	defer tx.Rollback()
    91→
    92→	// Get next version number
    93→	var maxVersion sql.NullInt64
    94→	err = tx.QueryRow(
    95→		`SELECT MAX(version) FROM skill_versions WHERE skill_name = ?`,
    96→		skillName,
    97→	).Scan(&maxVersion)
    98→	if err != nil {
    99→		return 0, fmt.Errorf("getting max version: %w", err)
   100→	}
   101→
   102→	nextVersion := 1
   103→	if maxVersion.Valid {
   104→		nextVersion = int(maxVersion.Int64) + 1
   105→	}
   106→
   107→	// Deactivate all previous versions
   108→	_, err = tx.Exec(
   109→		`UPDATE skill_versions SET active = 0 WHERE skill_name = ?`,
   110→		skillName,
   111→	)
   112→	if err != nil {
   113→		return 0, fmt.Errorf("deactivating versions: %w", err)
   114→	}
   115→
   116→	// Insert new version as active
   117→	_, err = tx.Exec(
   118→		`INSERT INTO skill_versions (skill_name, version, content, email_count, learning_count, active) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, 1)`,
   119→		skillName, nextVersion, content, emailCount, learningCount,
   120→	)
   121→	if err != nil {
   122→		return 0, fmt.Errorf("inserting version: %w", err)
   123→	}
   124→
   125→	if err := tx.Commit(); err != nil {
   126→		return 0, fmt.Errorf("committing transaction: %w", err)
   127→	}
   128→
   129→	return nextVersion, nil
   130→}
   131→
   132→// ListSkills returns a summary of all skills with their active version info.
   133→func ListSkills(db *sql.DB) ([]SkillSummary, error) {
   134→	rows, err := db.Query(`
   135→		SELECT
   136→			sv.skill_name,
   137→			COALESCE(active.version, 0) AS active_version,
   138→			COUNT(*) AS total_versions,
   139→			MAX(sv.created_at) AS last_updated
   140→		FROM skill_versions sv
   141→		LEFT JOIN skill_versions active
   142→			ON active.skill_name = sv.skill_name AND active.active = 1
   143→		GROUP BY sv.skill_name
   144→		ORDER BY sv.skill_name
   145→	`)
   146→	if err != nil {
   147→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("listing skills: %w", err)
   148→	}
   149→	defer rows.Close()
   150→
   151→	var skills []SkillSummary
   152→	for rows.Next() {
   153→		var s SkillSummary
   154→		if err := rows.Scan(&s.SkillName, &s.ActiveVersion, &s.TotalVersions, &s.LastUpdated); err != nil {
   155→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("scanning skill: %w", err)
   156→		}
   157→		skills = append(skills, s)
   158→	}
   159→	return skills, rows.Err()
   160→}
   161→
   162→// SkillHistory returns all versions of a skill, newest first.
   163→func SkillHistory(db *sql.DB, skillName string) ([]SkillVersion, error) {
   164→	rows, err := db.Query(
   165→		`SELECT id, skill_name, version, content, email_count, learning_count, active, created_at
   166→		 FROM skill_versions WHERE skill_name = ? ORDER BY version DESC`,
   167→		skillName,
   168→	)
   169→	if err != nil {
   170→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("getting skill history: %w", err)
   171→	}
   172→	defer rows.Close()
   173→
   174→	var versions []SkillVersion
   175→	for rows.Next() {
   176→		var v SkillVersion
   177→		if err := rows.Scan(&v.ID, &v.SkillName, &v.Version, &v.Content, &v.EmailCount, &v.LearningCount, &v.Active, &v.CreatedAt); err != nil {
   178→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("scanning version: %w", err)
   179→		}
   180→		versions = append(versions, v)
   181→	}
   182→	return versions, rows.Err()
   183→}
   184→
   185→// RollbackSkill restores a previous version of a skill by marking it as active
   186→// and writing its content to disk.
   187→func RollbackSkill(db *sql.DB, skillName string, version int) error {
   188→	tx, err := db.Begin()
   189→	if err != nil {
   190→		return fmt.Errorf("beginning transaction: %w", err)
   191→	}
   192→	defer tx.Rollback()
   193→
   194→	// Verify the version exists
   195→	var content string
   196→	err = tx.QueryRow(
   197→		`SELECT content FROM skill_versions WHERE skill_name = ? AND version = ?`,
   198→		skillName, version,
   199→	).Scan(&content)
   200→	if err == sql.ErrNoRows {
   201→		return fmt.Errorf("version %d not found for skill %s", version, skillName)
   202→	}
   203→	if err != nil {
   204→		return fmt.Errorf("reading version: %w", err)
   205→	}
   206→
   207→	// Deactivate all versions
   208→	_, err = tx.Exec(
   209→		`UPDATE skill_versions SET active = 0 WHERE skill_name = ?`,
   210→		skillName,
   211→	)
   212→	if err != nil {
   213→		return fmt.Errorf("deactivating versions: %w", err)
   214→	}
   215→
   216→	// Activate the target version
   217→	_, err = tx.Exec(
   218→		`UPDATE skill_versions SET active = 1 WHERE skill_name = ? AND version = ?`,
   219→		skillName, version,
   220→	)
   221→	if err != nil {
   222→		return fmt.Errorf("activating version: %w", err)
   223→	}
   224→
   225→	if err := tx.Commit(); err != nil {
   226→		return fmt.Errorf("committing transaction: %w", err)
   227→	}
   228→
   229→	// Write to disk
   230→	skillDir := filepath.Join(".claude", "skills", skillName)
   231→	if err := os.MkdirAll(skillDir, 0755); err != nil {
   232→		return fmt.Errorf("creating skill directory: %w", err)
   233→	}
   234→	skillPath := filepath.Join(skillDir, "SKILL.md")
   235→	if err := os.WriteFile(skillPath, []byte(content), 0644); err != nil {
   236→		return fmt.Errorf("writing skill file: %w", err)
   237→	}
   238→
   239→	return nil
   240→}
   241→
   242→// PrintJSON marshals v to JSON and writes it to stdout.
   243→func PrintJSON(v any) error {
   244→	enc := json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout)
   245→	enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
   246→	return enc.Encode(v)
   247→}

1→---
     2→name: nurge-setup
     3→description: Interactive setup for nurge — understands your company, sets outbound KPIs, connects tools, and generates personalized skills. Run after `nurge`.
     4→version: 4.0.0
     5→---
     6→
     7→# Nurge Setup (Router)
     8→
     9→You are helping the user set up nurge for outbound sales. This is a conversational onboarding — ask one question at a time, be warm and direct, use the user's name throughout.
    10→
    11→**This file is the router.** It runs prerequisites, checks resume state, runs Phase 1, detects maturity, and delegates to the right Phase 2 file.
    12→
    13→## Prerequisites
    14→
    15→1. Confirm `nurge` has been run (check for `.nurge/` directory). If missing, tell the user to run `nurge` first.
    16→2. Run `nurge connectors` to see what's already connected.
    17→
    18→## Resume Logic
    19→
    20→Setup can be interrupted and resumed. Before starting, check what's already configured:
    21→
    22→Run: `nurge config show --format json`
    23→
    24→### If `setup_completed` is true:
    25→Say: "[Name], setup is already complete. What would you like to do?"
    26→Do NOT re-run setup.
    27→
    28→### If `maturity.level` is set:
    29→Phase 1 is done. Read the right phase-2 file and continue from there.
    30→Say: "[Name], welcome back! We figured out where you are — let me pick up from there."
    31→Then: Read `~/.claude/skills/nurge-setup/phase-2-{maturity.level}.md` and follow it.
    32→
    33→### If `maturity.level` is NOT set:
    34→Phase 1 is incomplete. Check Phase 1 config keys and skip completed steps.
    35→Read `~/.claude/skills/nurge-setup/phase-1-foundation.md` and follow it.
    36→
    37→### Phase 1 Resume Keys
    38→
    39→| Config key exists | Skip step |
    40→|-------------------|-----------|
    41→| `user.name` | F1 |
    42→| `company.website` | F2 |
    43→| `user.role` AND `team.size` | F3 |
    44→| `tools.crm` | F4 |
    45→| `email.connected` or Gmail in `nurge connectors` | F5 |
    46→| `detection.outbound_count` | F6 |
    47→| `maturity.level` | F7 (entire detection) |
    48→
    49→If resuming mid-Phase 1, greet with: "[Name], welcome back! Let me pick up where we left off."
    50→
    51→## Flow
    52→
    53→1. Read `~/.claude/skills/nurge-setup/phase-1-foundation.md` and follow it (steps F1–F7)
    54→2. Phase 1 ends with maturity detection (F7) — the level is saved to `maturity.level`
    55→3. Based on the detected level, read the corresponding Phase 2 file:
    56→   - `zero` → Read `~/.claude/skills/nurge-setup/phase-2-zero.md`
    57→   - `experimenting` → Read `~/.claude/skills/nurge-setup/phase-2-experimenting.md`
    58→   - `operating` → Read `~/.claude/skills/nurge-setup/phase-2-operating.md`
    59→   - `scaling` → Read `~/.claude/skills/nurge-setup/phase-2-scaling.md`
    60→4. Follow the Phase 2 file exactly.
    61→
    62→## Important Notes
    63→
    64→- **One question at a time.** Never dump multiple questions. This is a conversation, not a form.
    65→- **Use their name.** Make it personal throughout.
    66→- **Be honest about limitations.** Only email channel. Only Gmail + HubSpot connectors. Don't oversell.
    67→- **Save everything to config.** Every decision → `nurge config set`. Skills read config, not session logs.
    68→- **Session hooks capture the conversation automatically.** No extra work needed for learning.
    69→- **Show only summaries during classification.** Don't show raw classification tables — just the summary count.
    70→
    71→## Current Capabilities (Be Honest)
    72→
    73→- **Channels:** Email only. LinkedIn and phone on roadmap.
    74→- **Connectors:** Gmail (Tier 1) and HubSpot (Tier 2) only.
    75→- Be upfront about these limits. Don't ask about what we can't deliver.
    76→

1→# Skill Generation Guide
     2→
     3→This guide is used by all Phase 2 flows to generate the 16 project-local skills. Read this file when you reach the skill generation step (Z11, E14, O12, or S13).
     4→
     5→## File Structure
     6→
     7→Each skill MUST be a directory containing a SKILL.md file. Use the EXACT paths below. Do NOT use flat .md files. Do NOT rename skills.
     8→
     9→```
    10→.claude/skills/nurge-product/SKILL.md

    22→.claude/skills/nurge-hygiene/SKILL.md
    23→.claude/skills/nurge-sending-health/SKILL.md
    24→.claude/skills/nurge-analytics/SKILL.md
    25→.claude/skills/nurge-review/SKILL.md
    26→```
    27→
    28→That is 16 skills. The 17th is nurge-setup (global, already installed at `~/.claude/skills/nurge-setup/SKILL.md` — do not regenerate it).
    29→
    30→## YAML Frontmatter
    31→
    32→Every SKILL.md must have:
    33→
    34→```yaml
    35→---
    36→name: nurge-<name>  # must match directory name
    37→description: <one-line description>
    38→version: 1.0.0
    39→---
    40→```
    41→
    42→## Quality by Maturity Level
    43→
    44→Read `nurge config show --format json` and check `maturity.level`:
    45→
    46→- **zero:** Skills are conversation-derived. Acknowledge data will improve them. Use phrases like "based on what we discussed" and "this will sharpen as you send more emails."
    47→- **experimenting:** Hybrid. Use available data, supplement with assumptions. Reference specific patterns where data exists, note where assumptions fill gaps.
    48→- **operating:** Data-derived. Reference specific patterns from email analysis. Include actual metrics where available (reply rates, best personas, etc.).
    49→- **scaling:** Data-derived + team-aware. Reference team structure, territories, per-rep patterns. Include team benchmarks and cross-rep insights.
    50→
    51→## Per-Skill Instructions
    52→
    53→For each skill, reference `docs/skills.md` for full specification. Key highlights below:
    54→
    55→### nurge-outbound (CRITICAL)
    56→
    57→- Emails MUST be 50-90 words max
    58→- Structure: 1 hook → 1-2 value sentences → 1 CTA
    59→- No filler, no preambles, no feature lists
    60→- Include word count validation: count words before presenting, cut if over 90
    61→- If social proof exists (check `product.social_proof` config): weave ONE relevant proof point naturally. Never fabricate.
    62→- If no social proof: skip entirely — never invent proof.
    63→- Match tone to persona (technical for CTOs, business for VPs)
    64→- Include compliance requirements based on `prospects.geography`
    65→- Config: `product.positioning`, `personas`, `prospects.geography`, `sending.*`
    66→
    67→### nurge-product
    68→
    69→- Company knowledge base for all outbound drafting
    70→- Config: `company.*`, `product.positioning`, `product.social_proof`
    71→- Include website data saved during setup
    72→- For operating/scaling: include how they describe product in emails vs website
    73→
    74→### nurge-icp
    75→
    76→- Ideal customer profile — who to target
    77→- Config: `icp.*`, `prospects.geography`
    78→- For zero/experimenting: assumption-based, note it will improve with data
    79→- For operating/scaling: include data-derived insights (who they actually email vs who responds)
    80→
    81→### nurge-personas
    82→
    83→- Buyer personas — who to message within target companies
    84→- Config: `personas` (JSON array)
    85→- Include per-persona: title, pain points, messaging angle, tone preference
    86→- For operating/scaling: include data on which personas convert best
    87→
    88→### nurge-research
    89→
    90→- Deep-dive research on a target company or prospect
    91→- Multi-layered strategy: web search → company website → enrichment → public profiles → prior interactions
    92→- Geography-aware legal pages (France: /mentionslegales, Germany: /impressum)
    93→- Dispatch 5 parallel agents for comprehensive research
    94→- Config: `icp.*`, `personas`, `product.positioning`, `prospects.geography`
    95→
    96→### nurge-qualify
    97→
    98→- Evaluate lead fit and classify: prospect / competitor / partnership
    99→- Apply qualification framework from config
   100→- Config: `qualification.framework`, `icp.*`, `personas`, `company.description`, `company.industry`
   101→
   102→### nurge-pipeline
   103→
   104→- Orchestrates full outbound workflow
   105→- Manages prospect pipeline stages: new → researched → qualified → sequencing → replied → meeting → deal
   106→- Enforces daily sending limits from KPIs
   107→- Config: `kpi.*`, `sending.*`
   108→
   109→### nurge-sequence
   110→
   111→- Multi-step outreach cadences
   112→- Vary messaging per step (new angle each touch)
   113→- For zero/experimenting: 3-touch best-practice frameworks
   114→- For operating/scaling: informed by real performance data
   115→- Config: `personas`, `kpi.*`, `sending.*`
   116→
   117→### nurge-followup
   118→
   119→- Follow-up emails that add new value (not "just checking in")
   120→- Reference original email, escalate angle
   121→- Config: `product.positioning`, `personas`
   122→
   123→### nurge-replies
   124→
   125→- Handle incoming replies: interested, objection, not now, not interested, referral
   126→- Draft appropriate response per intent
   127→- Config: `product.positioning`, `personas`, `qualification.framework`
   128→
   129→### nurge-book
   130→
   131→- Convert positive replies into booked meetings
   132→- Propose specific times, handle timezone, prep meeting brief
   133→- Config: `personas`, `product.positioning`
   134→
   135→### nurge-prospects
   136→
   137→- Build and manage prospect lists — this IS the CRM for zero/experimenting users
   138→- Track prospect status, manage suppression, deduplicate
   139→- Config: `icp.*`, `personas`, `prospects.geography`
   140→- Critical for zero users who have no other system
   141→
   142→### nurge-hygiene
   143→
   144→- Data cleanliness and compliance
   145→- Flag stale prospects, bounced emails, duplicates
   146→- Config: `prospects.geography`
   147→
   148→### nurge-sending-health
   149→
   150→- Monitor sending infrastructure, manage warmup
   151→- DNS checks, bounce rate monitoring, warmup schedule
   152→- Warmup schedule: Week 1-2 (5/day) → Week 3-4 (10-15/day) → Week 5+ (20-30/day)
   153→- Config: `sending.*`, `kpi.daily_emails`
   154→
   155→### nurge-analytics
   156→
   157→- Track performance against KPI targets
   158→- Dashboard: emails sent, reply rate, meetings vs targets
   159→- For operating/scaling: full breakdown by persona, segment, sequence
   160→- Config: `kpi.*`
   161→
   162→### nurge-review
   163→
   164→- Periodic coaching — weekly/monthly KPI review
   165→- Pull progress, highlight wins, flag problems, suggest actions
   166→- For scaling: include per-rep breakdowns
   167→- Config: `kpi.*`, `user.name`, `personas`
   168→
   169→## Config Each Skill Reads
   170→
   171→| Skill | Config Keys |
   172→|-------|-------------|
   173→| nurge-product | `company.*`, `product.positioning`, `product.social_proof` |
   174→| nurge-icp | `icp.*`, `prospects.geography` |
   175→| nurge-personas | `personas` |
   176→| nurge-research | `icp.*`, `personas`, `product.positioning`, `prospects.geography` |
   177→| nurge-qualify | `qualification.framework`, `icp.*`, `personas`, `company.*` |
   178→| nurge-pipeline | `kpi.*`, `sending.*` |
   179→| nurge-outbound | `product.positioning`, `product.social_proof`, `personas`, `prospects.geography`, `sending.*` |
   180→| nurge-sequence | `personas`, `kpi.*`, `sending.*` |
   181→| nurge-followup | `product.positioning`, `personas` |
   182→| nurge-replies | `product.positioning`, `personas`, `qualification.framework` |
   183→| nurge-book | `personas`, `product.positioning` |
   184→| nurge-prospects | `icp.*`, `personas`, `prospects.geography` |
   185→| nurge-hygiene | `prospects.geography` |
   186→| nurge-sending-health | `sending.*`, `kpi.daily_emails` |
   187→| nurge-analytics | `kpi.*` |
   188→| nurge-review | `kpi.*`, `user.name`, `personas` |
   189→
   190→## CLI Commands Each Skill Uses
   191→
   192→| Skill | CLI Commands |
   193→|-------|-------------|
   194→| nurge-product | `nurge config show` |
   195→| nurge-icp | `nurge config show` |
   196→| nurge-personas | `nurge config show` |
   197→| nurge-research | `nurge emails list --format json` |
   198→| nurge-qualify | `nurge emails list --format json` |
   199→| nurge-pipeline | `nurge emails list`, `nurge emails stats`, `nurge config show` |
   200→| nurge-outbound | `nurge send --to <email> --subject <s> --body <b>` |
   201→| nurge-sequence | `nurge emails list`, `nurge send` |
   202→| nurge-followup | `nurge emails list --format json`, `nurge send` |
   203→| nurge-replies | `nurge emails list --format json`, `nurge send` |
   204→| nurge-book | `nurge send` |
   205→| nurge-prospects | `nurge emails list --format json`, `nurge emails stats` |
   206→| nurge-hygiene | `nurge emails list`, `nurge emails stats` |
   207→| nurge-sending-health | `nurge emails stats`, `dig TXT` |
   208→| nurge-analytics | `nurge emails stats`, `nurge emails list`, `nurge context generate` |
   209→| nurge-review | `nurge emails stats`, `nurge context generate`, `nurge learn list` |
   210→
   211→## What To Include In Every Skill
   212→
   213→1. **CLI commands** the skill uses (with examples)
   214→2. **Config keys** it reads (via `nurge config show --format json`)
   215→3. **How to invoke related skills** (e.g., nurge-outbound references nurge-research for prospect research)
   216→4. **Maturity-aware language** — check `maturity.level` and adjust confidence level of recommendations
   217→5. **One question at a time** — conversational, not form-like
   218→6. **Use the user's name** — read from `user.name` config
   219→

1→package connector
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"fmt"
     5→	"os"
     6→)
     7→
     8→// LoadCredentials returns the raw credential bytes for a connector.
     9→// For google_json connectors, returns the Google JSON file.
    10→// For generic connectors, returns JSON with client_id and client_secret.
    11→//
    12→// Fallback chain: local file → bundled → error
    13→func LoadCredentials(connectorID string) ([]byte, error) {
    14→	// 1. Local file
    15→	localPath := fmt.Sprintf(".nurge/connectors/%s/credentials.json", connectorID)
    16→	if data, err := os.ReadFile(localPath); err == nil {
    17→		return data, nil
    18→	}
    19→
    20→	// 2. Bundled (only gmail has bundled creds)
    21→	bundledPath := fmt.Sprintf("definitions/%s_credentials.json", connectorID)
    22→	if data, err := bundledDefinitions.ReadFile(bundledPath); err == nil {
    23→		return data, nil
    24→	}
    25→
    26→	return nil, fmt.Errorf("no credentials found for connector %q", connectorID)
    27→}

    10→func ListReplyQueue(d *sql.DB, limit int) ([]ReplyEvent, error) {
    11→	query := `
    12→		SELECT id, enrollment_id, mailbox_id, thread_id, message_id, contact_email, classification,
    13→			status, assigned_to, subject, snippet, draft_reply, handled_at, occurred_at
    14→		FROM reply_events
    15→		WHERE status = 'open'
    16→		ORDER BY occurred_at DESC`
    17→	if limit > 0 {
    18→		query += fmt.Sprintf(" LIMIT %d", limit)
    19→	}
    20→
    21→	rows, err := d.Query(query)
    22→	if err != nil {
    23→		return nil, err
    24→	}
    25→	defer rows.Close()
    26→
    27→	var items []ReplyEvent
    28→	for rows.Next() {
    29→		var item ReplyEvent
    30→		if err := rows.Scan(
    31→			&item.ID, &item.EnrollmentID, &item.MailboxID, &item.ThreadID, &item.MessageID, &item.ContactEmail,
    32→			&item.Classification, &item.Status, &item.AssignedTo, &item.Subject, &item.Snippet, &item.DraftReply,
    33→			&item.HandledAt, &item.OccurredAt,
    34→		); err != nil {
    35→			return nil, err
    36→		}
    37→		item.NeedsAction = item.Status == "open"
    38→		items = append(items, item)
    39→	}
    40→	return items, rows.Err()
    41→}
    42→
    43→func MarkReplyHandled(d *sql.DB, replyID, assignedTo string) error {
    44→	replyID = strings.TrimSpace(replyID)
    45→	if replyID == "" {
    46→		return fmt.Errorf("reply ID is required")
    47→	}
    48→	_, err := d.Exec(`
    49→		UPDATE reply_events
    50→		SET status = 'handled',
    51→			assigned_to = CASE WHEN ? = '' THEN assigned_to ELSE ? END,
    52→			handled_at = ?,
    53→			created_at = created_at
    54→		WHERE id = ?`,
    55→		assignedTo, assignedTo, time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339), replyID,
    56→	)
    57→	return err
    58→}
    59→
    60→func ReopenReply(d *sql.DB, replyID string) error {
    61→	replyID = strings.TrimSpace(replyID)
    62→	if replyID == "" {
    63→		return fmt.Errorf("reply ID is required")
    64→	}
    65→	_, err := d.Exec(`
    66→		UPDATE reply_events
    67→		SET status = 'open',
    68→			handled_at = ''
    69→		WHERE id = ?`,
    70→		replyID,
    71→	)
    72→	return err
    73→}
    74→
    75→func SaveReplyDraft(d *sql.DB, replyID, draftReply, assignedTo string) (*ReplyEvent, error) {
    76→	replyID = strings.TrimSpace(replyID)
    77→	if replyID == "" {
    78→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("reply ID is required")
    79→	}
    80→	_, err := d.Exec(`
    81→		UPDATE reply_events
    82→		SET draft_reply = ?,
    83→			assigned_to = CASE WHEN ? = '' THEN assigned_to ELSE ? END
    84→		WHERE id = ?`,
    85→		draftReply, assignedTo, assignedTo, replyID,
    86→	)
    87→	if err != nil {
    88→		return nil, err
    89→	}
    90→	return replyEventByID(d, replyID)
    91→}
    92→
    93→func replyEventByID(d *sql.DB, replyID string) (*ReplyEvent, error) {
    94→	row := d.QueryRow(`
    95→		SELECT id, enrollment_id, mailbox_id, thread_id, message_id, contact_email, classification,
    96→			status, assigned_to, subject, snippet, draft_reply, handled_at, occurred_at
    97→		FROM reply_events
    98→		WHERE id = ?`,
    99→		replyID,
   100→	)
   101→	var item ReplyEvent
   102→	if err := row.Scan(
   103→		&item.ID, &item.EnrollmentID, &item.MailboxID, &item.ThreadID, &item.MessageID, &item.ContactEmail,
   104→		&item.Classification, &item.Status, &item.AssignedTo, &item.Subject, &item.Snippet, &item.DraftReply,
   105→		&item.HandledAt, &item.OccurredAt,
   106→	); err != nil {
   107→		if err == sql.ErrNoRows {
   108→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reply event %q not found", replyID)
   109→		}
   110→		return nil, err
   111→	}
   112→	item.NeedsAction = item.Status == "open"
   113→	return &item, nil
   114→}

    22→type ListOptions struct {
    23→	Class  string
    24→	Limit  int
    25→	Offset int
    26→}
    27→
    28→func List(d *sql.DB, opts ListOptions) ([]Email, error) {
    29→	query := "SELECT id, thread_id, subject, from_email, to_emails, cc_emails, date, snippet, class, class_confidence, classified_by FROM emails"
    30→	var args []any
    31→
    32→	if opts.Class != "" {
    33→		query += " WHERE class = ?"
    34→		args = append(args, opts.Class)
    35→	}
    36→
    37→	query += " ORDER BY date DESC"
    38→
    39→	if opts.Limit > 0 {
    40→		query += fmt.Sprintf(" LIMIT %d", opts.Limit)
    41→		if opts.Offset > 0 {
    42→			query += fmt.Sprintf(" OFFSET %d", opts.Offset)
    43→		}
    44→	}
    45→
    46→	rows, err := d.Query(query, args...)
    47→	if err != nil {
    48→		return nil, err
    49→	}
    50→	defer rows.Close()
    51→
    52→	var emailList []Email
    53→	for rows.Next() {
    54→		var e Email
    55→		var subject, ccEmails, snippet, classifiedBy sql.NullString
    56→		if err := rows.Scan(&e.ID, &e.ThreadID, &subject, &e.FromEmail, &e.ToEmails, &ccEmails, &e.Date, &snippet, &e.Class, &e.ClassConfidence, &classifiedBy); err != nil {
    57→			return nil, err
    58→		}
    59→		e.Subject = subject.String
    60→		e.CcEmails = ccEmails.String
    61→		e.Snippet = snippet.String
    62→		e.ClassifiedBy = classifiedBy.String
    63→		emailList = append(emailList, e)
    64→	}
    65→	return emailList, rows.Err()
    66→}

     3→import (
     4→	"database/sql"
     5→	"time"
     6→)
     7→
     8→type EmailStats struct {
     9→	Total        int            `json:"total"`
    10→	ByClass      map[string]int `json:"by_class"`
    11→	DateRange    DateRange      `json:"date_range"`
    12→	Unclassified int            `json:"unclassified"`
    13→}
    14→
    15→type DateRange struct {
    16→	Earliest string `json:"earliest"`
    17→	Latest   string `json:"latest"`
    18→}
    19→
    20→// WeeklyStats holds outbound activity for the current week (Monday to now).
    21→type WeeklyStats struct {
    22→	OutboundSent   int `json:"outbound_sent"`
    23→	UniqueContacts int `json:"unique_contacts"`
    24→}
    25→
    26→func Stats(d *sql.DB) (*EmailStats, error) {
    27→	stats := &EmailStats{ByClass: make(map[string]int)}
    28→
    29→	d.QueryRow("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM emails").Scan(&stats.Total)
    30→
    31→	rows, err := d.Query("SELECT class, COUNT(*) FROM emails GROUP BY class")
    32→	if err != nil {
    33→		return nil, err
    34→	}
    35→	defer rows.Close()
    36→
    37→	for rows.Next() {
    38→		var class string
    39→		var count int
    40→		rows.Scan(&class, &count)
    41→		stats.ByClass[class] = count
    42→		if class == "unclassified" {
    43→			stats.Unclassified = count
    44→		}
    45→	}
    46→
    47→	d.QueryRow("SELECT MIN(date), MAX(date) FROM emails").Scan(&stats.DateRange.Earliest, &stats.DateRange.Latest)
    48→
    49→	return stats, nil
    50→}
    51→
    52→// ThisWeek returns outbound email counts for the current week (since Monday).
    53→func ThisWeek(d *sql.DB) (*WeeklyStats, error) {
    54→	w := &WeeklyStats{}
    55→
    56→	now := time.Now()
    57→	weekday := int(now.Weekday())
    58→	if weekday == 0 {
    59→		weekday = 7 // Sunday = 7
    60→	}
    61→	monday := now.AddDate(0, 0, -(weekday - 1))
    62→	mondayStr := monday.Format("2006-01-02")
    63→
    64→	d.QueryRow(
    65→		"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM emails WHERE class = 'outbound' AND date >= ?",
    66→		mondayStr,
    67→	).Scan(&w.OutboundSent)
    68→
    69→	d.QueryRow(
    70→		"SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT to_emails) FROM emails WHERE class = 'outbound' AND date >= ?",
    71→		mondayStr,
    72→	).Scan(&w.UniqueContacts)
    73→
    74→	return w, nil
    75→}

   302→func BuiltInTemplates() []Template {
   303→	names := make([]string, 0, len(builtInTemplates))
   304→	for name := range builtInTemplates {
   305→		names = append(names, name)
   306→	}
   307→	sort.Strings(names)
   308→
   309→	templates := make([]Template, 0, len(names))
   310→	for _, name := range names {

   312→	}
   313→	return templates

   325→func EnsureMailbox(d *sql.DB, email, provider string) (*Mailbox, error) {
   326→	if email == "" {
   327→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("mailbox email is required")
   328→	}
   329→
   330→	_, err := d.Exec(`
   331→		INSERT INTO mailboxes (id, email, transport_email, provider, warmup_started_at)
   332→		VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, datetime('now'))
   333→		ON CONFLICT(email) DO UPDATE SET
   334→			provider = excluded.provider,
   335→			transport_email = CASE
   336→				WHEN mailboxes.transport_email = '' THEN excluded.transport_email
   337→				ELSE mailboxes.transport_email
   338→			END,
   339→			warmup_started_at = CASE
   340→				WHEN mailboxes.warmup_started_at = '' THEN excluded.warmup_started_at
   341→				ELSE mailboxes.warmup_started_at
   342→			END,
   343→			updated_at = datetime('now')`,
   344→		newID("mbx"), email, email, provider,
   345→	)
   346→	if err != nil {
   347→		return nil, err
   348→	}

1→package project
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"os"
     5→	"os/exec"
     6→)
     7→
     8→type gitStep struct{}
     9→
    10→func (s *gitStep) Name() string { return "Git repository initialized" }
    11→
    12→func (s *gitStep) Done() (bool, error) {
    13→	// Done if .git exists AND has at least one commit
    14→	if _, err := os.Stat(".git"); err != nil {
    15→		return false, nil
    16→	}
    17→	cmd := exec.Command("git", "rev-parse", "HEAD")
    18→	cmd.Stdout = nil
    19→	cmd.Stderr = nil
    20→	return cmd.Run() == nil, nil
    21→}
    22→
    23→func (s *gitStep) Run() error {
    24→	// Init if needed
    25→	if _, err := os.Stat(".git"); err != nil {
    26→		cmd := exec.Command("git", "init", "-q")
    27→		cmd.Stdout = os.Stdout
    28→		cmd.Stderr = os.Stderr
    29→		if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil {
    30→			return err
    31→		}
    32→	}
    33→
    34→	// Initial commit so Claude Code recognizes the project
    35→	add := exec.Command("git", "add", "-A")
    36→	add.Stdout = os.Stdout
    37→	add.Stderr = os.Stderr
    38→	if err := add.Run(); err != nil {
    39→		return err
    40→	}
    41→
    42→	commit := exec.Command("git", "commit", "-q", "-m", "nurge init")
    43→	commit.Stdout = os.Stdout
    44→	commit.Stderr = os.Stderr
    45→	return commit.Run()
    46→}
    47→

    10→type nextStepStep struct{}
    11→
    12→func (s *nextStepStep) Name() string { return "" } // no label, just prints
    13→
    14→func (s *nextStepStep) Done() (bool, error) { return false, nil }
    15→
    16→func (s *nextStepStep) Run() error {
    17→	fmt.Println()
    18→
    19→	if isInsideClaudeCode() {
    20→		fmt.Println("  You're ready. Run:")
    21→		fmt.Println("    /nurge:setup")
    22→		fmt.Println()
    23→		return nil
    24→	}
    25→
    26→	if isClaudeInstalled() {
    27→		fmt.Println("  Launching Claude Code...")
    28→		fmt.Println()
    29→		return launchClaude()
    30→	}
    31→
    32→	fmt.Println("  Almost there! Install Claude Code first:")
    33→	fmt.Println("    https://claude.ai/code")
    34→	fmt.Println()
    35→	fmt.Println("  Then run:")
    36→	fmt.Println("    claude -p \"/nurge:setup\"")
    37→	fmt.Println()
    38→	return nil
    39→}

    41→func isInsideClaudeCode() bool {
    42→	return os.Getenv("CLAUDE_CODE") != ""
    43→}
    44→
    45→func isClaudeInstalled() bool {
    46→	_, err := exec.LookPath("claude")
    47→	return err == nil
    48→}
    49→
    50→// launchClaude replaces the current process with claude in interactive mode.
    51→// SessionStart hooks handle the rest — banner shows status, prompt guides Claude.
    52→func launchClaude() error {
    53→	claudePath, err := exec.LookPath("claude")
    54→	if err != nil {
    55→		return fmt.Errorf("claude not found: %w", err)
    56→	}
    57→
    58→	// syscall.Exec replaces the nurge process with claude — clean handoff
    59→	return syscall.Exec(claudePath, []string{"claude"}, os.Environ())
    60→}

     6→	"fmt"
     7→	"os"
     8→	"path/filepath"
     9→)
    10→
    11→// Learning represents a single insight captured from a Claude Code session.
    12→type Learning struct {
    13→	ID            int    `json:"id"`
    14→	Type          string `json:"type"`
    15→	Content       string `json:"content"`
    16→	SourceSession string `json:"source_session,omitempty"`
    17→	CreatedAt     string `json:"created_at"`
    18→}
    19→
    20→// SkillVersion represents a versioned snapshot of a skill.
    21→type SkillVersion struct {
    22→	ID            int    `json:"id"`
    23→	SkillName     string `json:"skill_name"`
    24→	Version       int    `json:"version"`
    25→	Content       string `json:"content"`
    26→	EmailCount    int    `json:"email_count"`
    27→	LearningCount int    `json:"learning_count"`
    28→	Active        bool   `json:"active"`
    29→	CreatedAt     string `json:"created_at"`
    30→}
    31→
    32→// SkillSummary is a brief view of a skill with its current version.
    33→type SkillSummary struct {
    34→	SkillName      string `json:"skill_name"`
    35→	ActiveVersion  int    `json:"active_version"`
    36→	TotalVersions  int    `json:"total_versions"`
    37→	LastUpdated    string `json:"last_updated"`
    38→}
    39→
    40→// SaveLearning inserts a new learning into the database.
    41→func SaveLearning(db *sql.DB, typ, content, sourceSession string) (int64, error) {
    42→	result, err := db.Exec(
    43→		`INSERT INTO learnings (type, content, source_session) VALUES (?, ?, ?)`,
    44→		typ, content, sourceSession,
    45→	)
    46→	if err != nil {
    47→		return 0, fmt.Errorf("saving learning: %w", err)
    48→	}
    49→	return result.LastInsertId()
    50→}
    51→
    52→// ListLearnings returns all learnings, optionally filtered by type.
    53→func ListLearnings(db *sql.DB, filterType string) ([]Learning, error) {
    54→	var rows *sql.Rows
    55→	var err error
    56→
    57→	if filterType != "" {
    58→		rows, err = db.Query(
    59→			`SELECT id, type, content, source_session, created_at FROM learnings WHERE type = ? ORDER BY created_at DESC`,
    60→			filterType,
    61→		)
    62→	} else {
    63→		rows, err = db.Query(
    64→			`SELECT id, type, content, source_session, created_at FROM learnings ORDER BY created_at DESC`,
    65→		)
    66→	}
    67→	if err != nil {
    68→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("listing learnings: %w", err)
    69→	}
    70→	defer rows.Close()
    71→
    72→	var learnings []Learning
    73→	for rows.Next() {
    74→		var l Learning
    75→		if err := rows.Scan(&l.ID, &l.Type, &l.Content, &l.SourceSession, &l.CreatedAt); err != nil {
    76→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("scanning learning: %w", err)
    77→		}
    78→		learnings = append(learnings, l)
    79→	}
    80→	return learnings, rows.Err()
    81→}
    82→
    83→// SaveSkillVersion saves a new version of a skill. It deactivates all previous
    84→// versions and marks the new one as active.
    85→func SaveSkillVersion(db *sql.DB, skillName, content string, emailCount, learningCount int) (int, error) {
    86→	tx, err := db.Begin()
    87→	if err != nil {
    88→		return 0, fmt.Errorf("beginning transaction: %w", err)
    89→	}
    90→	defer tx.Rollback()
    91→
    92→	// Get next version number
    93→	var maxVersion sql.NullInt64
    94→	err = tx.QueryRow(
    95→		`SELECT MAX(version) FROM skill_versions WHERE skill_name = ?`,
    96→		skillName,
    97→	).Scan(&maxVersion)
    98→	if err != nil {
    99→		return 0, fmt.Errorf("getting max version: %w", err)
   100→	}
   101→
   102→	nextVersion := 1
   103→	if maxVersion.Valid {
   104→		nextVersion = int(maxVersion.Int64) + 1
   105→	}
   106→
   107→	// Deactivate all previous versions
   108→	_, err = tx.Exec(
   109→		`UPDATE skill_versions SET active = 0 WHERE skill_name = ?`,
   110→		skillName,
   111→	)
   112→	if err != nil {
   113→		return 0, fmt.Errorf("deactivating versions: %w", err)
   114→	}
   115→
   116→	// Insert new version as active
   117→	_, err = tx.Exec(
   118→		`INSERT INTO skill_versions (skill_name, version, content, email_count, learning_count, active) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, 1)`,
   119→		skillName, nextVersion, content, emailCount, learningCount,
   120→	)
   121→	if err != nil {
   122→		return 0, fmt.Errorf("inserting version: %w", err)
   123→	}
   124→
   125→	if err := tx.Commit(); err != nil {
   126→		return 0, fmt.Errorf("committing transaction: %w", err)
   127→	}
   128→
   129→	return nextVersion, nil
   130→}
   131→
   132→// ListSkills returns a summary of all skills with their active version info.
   133→func ListSkills(db *sql.DB) ([]SkillSummary, error) {
   134→	rows, err := db.Query(`
   135→		SELECT
   136→			sv.skill_name,
   137→			COALESCE(active.version, 0) AS active_version,
   138→			COUNT(*) AS total_versions,
   139→			MAX(sv.created_at) AS last_updated
   140→		FROM skill_versions sv
   141→		LEFT JOIN skill_versions active
   142→			ON active.skill_name = sv.skill_name AND active.active = 1
   143→		GROUP BY sv.skill_name
   144→		ORDER BY sv.skill_name
   145→	`)
   146→	if err != nil {
   147→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("listing skills: %w", err)
   148→	}
   149→	defer rows.Close()
   150→
   151→	var skills []SkillSummary
   152→	for rows.Next() {
   153→		var s SkillSummary
   154→		if err := rows.Scan(&s.SkillName, &s.ActiveVersion, &s.TotalVersions, &s.LastUpdated); err != nil {
   155→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("scanning skill: %w", err)
   156→		}
   157→		skills = append(skills, s)
   158→	}
   159→	return skills, rows.Err()
   160→}
   161→
   162→// SkillHistory returns all versions of a skill, newest first.
   163→func SkillHistory(db *sql.DB, skillName string) ([]SkillVersion, error) {
   164→	rows, err := db.Query(
   165→		`SELECT id, skill_name, version, content, email_count, learning_count, active, created_at
   166→		 FROM skill_versions WHERE skill_name = ? ORDER BY version DESC`,
   167→		skillName,
   168→	)
   169→	if err != nil {
   170→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("getting skill history: %w", err)
   171→	}
   172→	defer rows.Close()
   173→
   174→	var versions []SkillVersion
   175→	for rows.Next() {
   176→		var v SkillVersion
   177→		if err := rows.Scan(&v.ID, &v.SkillName, &v.Version, &v.Content, &v.EmailCount, &v.LearningCount, &v.Active, &v.CreatedAt); err != nil {
   178→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("scanning version: %w", err)
   179→		}
   180→		versions = append(versions, v)
   181→	}
   182→	return versions, rows.Err()
   183→}
   184→
   185→// RollbackSkill restores a previous version of a skill by marking it as active
   186→// and writing its content to disk.
   187→func RollbackSkill(db *sql.DB, skillName string, version int) error {
   188→	tx, err := db.Begin()
   189→	if err != nil {
   190→		return fmt.Errorf("beginning transaction: %w", err)
   191→	}
   192→	defer tx.Rollback()
   193→
   194→	// Verify the version exists
   195→	var content string
   196→	err = tx.QueryRow(
   197→		`SELECT content FROM skill_versions WHERE skill_name = ? AND version = ?`,
   198→		skillName, version,
   199→	).Scan(&content)
   200→	if err == sql.ErrNoRows {
   201→		return fmt.Errorf("version %d not found for skill %s", version, skillName)
   202→	}
   203→	if err != nil {
   204→		return fmt.Errorf("reading version: %w", err)
   205→	}
   206→
   207→	// Deactivate all versions
   208→	_, err = tx.Exec(
   209→		`UPDATE skill_versions SET active = 0 WHERE skill_name = ?`,
   210→		skillName,
   211→	)
   212→	if err != nil {
   213→		return fmt.Errorf("deactivating versions: %w", err)
   214→	}
   215→
   216→	// Activate the target version
   217→	_, err = tx.Exec(
   218→		`UPDATE skill_versions SET active = 1 WHERE skill_name = ? AND version = ?`,
   219→		skillName, version,
   220→	)
   221→	if err != nil {
   222→		return fmt.Errorf("activating version: %w", err)
   223→	}
   224→
   225→	if err := tx.Commit(); err != nil {
   226→		return fmt.Errorf("committing transaction: %w", err)
   227→	}
   228→
   229→	// Write to disk
   230→	skillDir := filepath.Join(".claude", "skills", skillName)
   231→	if err := os.MkdirAll(skillDir, 0755); err != nil {
   232→		return fmt.Errorf("creating skill directory: %w", err)
   233→	}
   234→	skillPath := filepath.Join(skillDir, "SKILL.md")
   235→	if err := os.WriteFile(skillPath, []byte(content), 0644); err != nil {
   236→		return fmt.Errorf("writing skill file: %w", err)
   237→	}
   238→
   239→	return nil
   240→}
   241→
   242→// PrintJSON marshals v to JSON and writes it to stdout.
   243→func PrintJSON(v any) error {
   244→	enc := json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout)
   245→	enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
   246→	return enc.Encode(v)
   247→}

1→package session
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"bufio"
     5→	"log/slog"
     6→	"os"
     7→	"path/filepath"
     8→	"sync"
     9→)
    10→
    11→const (
    12→	logDir  = ".nurge/sessions"
    13→	logFile = ".nurge/sessions/nurge.log"
    14→	bufSize = 8192 // 8KB buffer
    15→)
    16→
    17→var (
    18→	loggerOnce sync.Once
    19→	logger     *slog.Logger
    20→	logWriter  *bufio.Writer
    21→	logFD      *os.File
    22→)
    23→
    24→// Logger returns a shared slog.Logger that writes JSON to .nurge/sessions/nurge.log.
    25→// Safe to call from hooks — creates the file and directory if needed.
    26→// Returns a no-op logger if the file cannot be opened.
    27→func Logger() *slog.Logger {
    28→	loggerOnce.Do(func() {
    29→		if err := os.MkdirAll(logDir, 0755); err != nil {
    30→			logger = slog.New(slog.NewJSONHandler(os.Stderr, nil))
    31→			return
    32→		}
    33→
    34→		f, err := os.OpenFile(logFile, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_APPEND, 0644)
    35→		if err != nil {
    36→			logger = slog.New(slog.NewJSONHandler(os.Stderr, nil))
    37→			return
    38→		}
    39→
    40→		logFD = f
    41→		logWriter = bufio.NewWriterSize(f, bufSize)
    42→		logger = slog.New(slog.NewJSONHandler(logWriter, &slog.HandlerOptions{
    43→			Level: slog.LevelInfo,
    44→		}))
    45→	})
    46→	return logger
    47→}
    48→
    49→// FlushLog flushes the buffered log writer. Call before process exit.
    50→func FlushLog() {
    51→	if logWriter != nil {
    52→		logWriter.Flush()
    53→	}
    54→}
    55→
    56→// TmpDir returns the path to the sessions temp directory.
    57→func TmpDir() string {
    58→	return filepath.Join(logDir, "tmp")
    59→}
    60→

    12→
    13→// User represents an authenticated Nurge user.
    14→type User struct {
    15→	Email string `json:"email"`
    16→	Name  string `json:"name"`
    17→}
    18→
    19→// ConvexClient calls Convex HTTP actions for auth operations.
    20→type ConvexClient struct {
    21→	baseURL    string
    22→	httpClient *http.Client
    23→}
    24→
    25→// NewConvexClient creates a client for the given Convex site URL.
    26→func NewConvexClient(baseURL string) *ConvexClient {
    27→	return &ConvexClient{
    28→		baseURL: baseURL,
    29→		httpClient: &http.Client{
    30→			Timeout: 10 * time.Second,
    31→		},
    32→	}
    33→}
    34→
    35→type validateResponse struct {
    36→	Valid bool  `json:"valid"`
    37→	User  *User `json:"user,omitempty"`
    38→}
    39→
    40→// ErrTokenInvalid is returned when the server confirms the token is invalid.
    41→// This is distinct from network errors, which may allow grace period fallback.
    42→var ErrTokenInvalid = errors.New("token is invalid or expired")
    43→
    44→// ValidateToken checks a token against the Convex backend.
    45→// Returns ErrTokenInvalid if server says token is invalid.
    46→// Returns a different error for network/parse failures.
    47→func (c *ConvexClient) ValidateToken(token string) (*User, error) {
    48→	body, err := json.Marshal(map[string]string{"token": token})
    49→	if err != nil {
    50→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("marshaling request: %w", err)
    51→	}
    52→	resp, err := c.httpClient.Post(
    53→		c.baseURL+"/api/validate-token",
    54→		"application/json",
    55→		bytes.NewReader(body),
    56→	)
    57→	if err != nil {
    58→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("calling validate-token: %w", err)
    59→	}
    60→	defer resp.Body.Close()
    61→
    62→	data, err := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)
    63→	if err != nil {
    64→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("reading response: %w", err)
    65→	}
    66→
    67→	var result validateResponse
    68→	if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &result); err != nil {
    69→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("parsing response: %w", err)
    70→	}
    71→
    72→	if !result.Valid {
    73→		return nil, ErrTokenInvalid
    74→	}
    75→	return result.User, nil
    76→}

    86→func (c *ConvexClient) ExchangeCode(code string) (*exchangeResponse, error) {
    87→	body, _ := json.Marshal(map[string]string{"code": code})

    10→// HookInput is the generic structure for all hook stdin payloads.
    11→// Claude Code sends JSON on stdin to hooks. Fields vary by event type.
    12→type HookInput struct {
    13→	SessionID      string `json:"session_id"`
    14→	Model          string `json:"model,omitempty"`
    15→	Source         string `json:"source,omitempty"`
    16→	TranscriptPath string `json:"transcript_path,omitempty"`
    17→
    18→	// UserPromptSubmit fields
    19→	Prompt string `json:"prompt,omitempty"`
    20→
    21→	// Stop fields
    22→	StopReason    string   `json:"stop_reason,omitempty"`
    23→	ModifiedFiles []string `json:"modified_files,omitempty"`
    24→}
    25→
    26→// ReadStdin reads and parses hook JSON from stdin.
    27→func ReadStdin() (*HookInput, error) {
    28→	data, err := io.ReadAll(os.Stdin)
    29→	if err != nil {
    30→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("reading stdin: %w", err)
    31→	}
    32→	if len(data) == 0 {
    33→		return &HookInput{}, nil
    34→	}
    35→
    36→	var input HookInput
    37→	if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &input); err != nil {
    38→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("parsing hook input: %w", err)
    39→	}
    40→	return &input, nil
    41→}

    63→func RecordSend(d *sql.DB, mailboxID, sendType string, now time.Time) error {
    64→	sendType = NormalizeSendType(sendType)
    65→	if sendType == SendTypeManual {
    66→		return nil
    67→	}
    68→	if now.IsZero() {
    69→		now = time.Now()
    70→	}
    71→
    72→	_, err := d.Exec(`
    73→		INSERT INTO mailbox_daily_usage (
    74→			mailbox_id, usage_day, first_touches_sent, follow_ups_sent, replies_sent
    75→		) VALUES (?, ?, 0, 0, 0)
    76→		ON CONFLICT(mailbox_id, usage_day) DO NOTHING`,
    77→		mailboxID, dayKey(now),
    78→	)
    79→	if err != nil {
    80→		return err
    81→	}
    82→
    83→	column := usageColumn(sendType)
    84→	if column != "" {
    85→		if _, err := d.Exec(
    86→			fmt.Sprintf(`UPDATE mailbox_daily_usage SET %s = %s + 1 WHERE mailbox_id = ? AND usage_day = ?`, column, column),
    87→			mailboxID, dayKey(now),
    88→		); err != nil {
    89→			return err
    90→		}
    91→	}
    92→
    93→	if _, err := d.Exec(`
    94→		INSERT INTO mailbox_send_events (id, mailbox_id, send_type, sent_at)
    95→		VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)`,
    96→		newID("mse"), mailboxID, sendType, now.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
    97→	); err != nil {
    98→		return err
    99→	}
   100→
   101→	_, err = d.Exec(`UPDATE mailboxes SET last_used_at = ?, updated_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?`, now.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339), mailboxID)
   102→	return err
   103→}

   105→func LogSendAttempt(d *sql.DB, opts SendAttemptOptions) error {
   106→	if opts.MailboxID == "" {
   107→		return fmt.Errorf("mailbox ID is required")
   108→	}
   109→	if strings.TrimSpace(opts.ContactEmail) == "" {
   110→		return fmt.Errorf("contact email is required")
   111→	}
   112→	if strings.TrimSpace(opts.Status) == "" {
   113→		return fmt.Errorf("send attempt status is required")
   114→	}
   115→	if opts.AttemptedAt.IsZero() {
   116→		opts.AttemptedAt = time.Now()
   117→	}
   118→
   119→	_, err := d.Exec(`
   120→		INSERT INTO send_attempts (
   121→			id, mailbox_id, enrollment_id, task_id, contact_email, send_type,
   122→			status, subject, error, message_id, provider_id, attempted_at
   123→		) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
   124→		newID("send"), opts.MailboxID, opts.EnrollmentID, opts.TaskID, strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(opts.ContactEmail)),
   125→		NormalizeSendType(opts.SendType), opts.Status, opts.Subject, opts.Error, opts.MessageID, opts.ProviderID,
   126→		opts.AttemptedAt.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
   127→	)
   128→	return err
   129→}

   131→func ListSendAttempts(d *sql.DB, limit int, status string) ([]SendAttempt, error) {
   132→	query := `
   133→		SELECT s.id, s.mailbox_id, m.email, s.enrollment_id, s.task_id, s.contact_email, s.send_type,
   134→			s.status, s.subject, s.error, s.message_id, s.provider_id, s.attempted_at
   135→		FROM send_attempts s
   136→		JOIN mailboxes m ON m.id = s.mailbox_id`
   137→	var args []any
   138→	if status != "" {
   139→		query += ` WHERE s.status = ?`
   140→		args = append(args, status)
   141→	}
   142→	query += ` ORDER BY s.attempted_at DESC`
   143→	if limit > 0 {
   144→		query += fmt.Sprintf(" LIMIT %d", limit)
   145→	}
   146→
   147→	rows, err := d.Query(query, args...)
   148→	if err != nil {
   149→		return nil, err
   150→	}
   151→	defer rows.Close()
   152→
   153→	var attempts []SendAttempt
   154→	for rows.Next() {
   155→		var item SendAttempt
   156→		if err := rows.Scan(
   157→			&item.ID, &item.MailboxID, &item.MailboxEmail, &item.EnrollmentID, &item.TaskID, &item.ContactEmail,
   158→			&item.SendType, &item.Status, &item.Subject, &item.Error, &item.MessageID, &item.ProviderID, &item.AttemptedAt,
   159→		); err != nil {
   160→			return nil, err
   161→		}
   162→		attempts = append(attempts, item)
   163→	}
   164→	return attempts, rows.Err()
   165→}

   167→func UpdateTaskSendState(d *sql.DB, taskID, sendStatus, messageID, providerID, lastError string) error {
   168→	sendStatus = strings.TrimSpace(sendStatus)
   169→	if sendStatus == "" {
   170→		sendStatus = TaskSendStatusPending
   171→	}
   172→	_, err := d.Exec(`
   173→		UPDATE follow_up_tasks
   174→		SET send_status = ?,
   175→			unipile_message_id = CASE WHEN ? = '' THEN unipile_message_id ELSE ? END,
   176→			provider_id = CASE WHEN ? = '' THEN provider_id ELSE ? END,
   177→			last_error = CASE WHEN ? = '' THEN last_error ELSE ? END,
   178→			updated_at = datetime('now')
   179→		WHERE id = ?`,
   180→		sendStatus,
   181→		messageID, messageID,
   182→		providerID, providerID,
   183→		lastError, lastError,
   184→		taskID,
   185→	)
   186→	return err
   187→}
   188→
   189→func UpsertDraftForTask(d execer, taskID, enrollmentID string, stepNumber int, subject, body, generatedBy, generatedContext string) error {
   190→	if taskID == "" || enrollmentID == "" {
   191→		return fmt.Errorf("task ID and enrollment ID are required")
   192→	}
   193→	_, err := d.Exec(`
   194→		INSERT INTO sequence_drafts (
   195→			id, enrollment_id, task_id, step_number, subject, body, channel, status, generated_by, generated_context
   196→		) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, 'email', 'pending', ?, ?)
   197→		ON CONFLICT(task_id) DO UPDATE SET
   198→			subject = excluded.subject,
   199→			body = excluded.body,
   200→			generated_by = excluded.generated_by,
   201→			generated_context = excluded.generated_context,
   202→			status = CASE
   203→				WHEN sequence_drafts.status = 'sent' THEN sequence_drafts.status
   204→				ELSE excluded.status
   205→			END,
   206→			updated_at = datetime('now')`,
   207→		newID("draft"), enrollmentID, taskID, stepNumber, subject, body, generatedBy, generatedContext,
   208→	)
   209→	return err
   210→}
   211→
   212→func DraftByTaskID(d *sql.DB, taskID string) (*SequenceDraft, error) {
   213→	row := d.QueryRow(`
   214→		SELECT id, enrollment_id, task_id, step_number, subject, body, channel, status, generated_by, generated_context
   215→		FROM sequence_drafts
   216→		WHERE task_id = ?`,
   217→		taskID,
   218→	)
   219→	var draft SequenceDraft
   220→	if err := row.Scan(
   221→		&draft.ID, &draft.EnrollmentID, &draft.TaskID, &draft.StepNumber, &draft.Subject, &draft.Body,
   222→		&draft.Channel, &draft.Status, &draft.GeneratedBy, &draft.GeneratedContext,
   223→	); err != nil {
   224→		if err == sql.ErrNoRows {
   225→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("draft for task %q not found", taskID)
   226→		}
   227→		return nil, err
   228→	}
   229→	return &draft, nil
   230→}

   232→func UpdateDraftStatusByTaskID(d execer, taskID, status string) error {
   233→	_, err := d.Exec(`UPDATE sequence_drafts SET status = ?, updated_at = datetime('now') WHERE task_id = ?`, status, taskID)

    10→func ListReplyQueue(d *sql.DB, limit int) ([]ReplyEvent, error) {
    11→	query := `
    12→		SELECT id, enrollment_id, mailbox_id, thread_id, message_id, contact_email, classification,
    13→			status, assigned_to, subject, snippet, draft_reply, handled_at, occurred_at
    14→		FROM reply_events
    15→		WHERE status = 'open'
    16→		ORDER BY occurred_at DESC`
    17→	if limit > 0 {
    18→		query += fmt.Sprintf(" LIMIT %d", limit)
    19→	}
    20→
    21→	rows, err := d.Query(query)
    22→	if err != nil {
    23→		return nil, err
    24→	}
    25→	defer rows.Close()
    26→
    27→	var items []ReplyEvent
    28→	for rows.Next() {
    29→		var item ReplyEvent
    30→		if err := rows.Scan(
    31→			&item.ID, &item.EnrollmentID, &item.MailboxID, &item.ThreadID, &item.MessageID, &item.ContactEmail,
    32→			&item.Classification, &item.Status, &item.AssignedTo, &item.Subject, &item.Snippet, &item.DraftReply,
    33→			&item.HandledAt, &item.OccurredAt,
    34→		); err != nil {
    35→			return nil, err
    36→		}
    37→		item.NeedsAction = item.Status == "open"
    38→		items = append(items, item)
    39→	}
    40→	return items, rows.Err()
    41→}
